title: Blade
review:
first watch of this and the movie so much better and funnier than i expected. it definitely seems like it’s supposed to come off corny and it gets that perfectly. wesley snipes himself is a really good blade, the story was good bar the weird incest undertones, and frost was a fine villain

Last Watched: March 24th, 2025

title: X-Men
review:
honestly super good, i thought every character on the surface level was shown what their motivations were and how’ll they’ll be. the rogue and wolverine dynamic was incredibly well done i thought, and very sweet. i feel like charles xavier’s casting and performance was downright perfect, and same with magneto. very very good movie to introduce you into some of the x-men (first watch)

Last Watched: March 25th, 2025

title: Blade 2
review:
the action is quite good and the villains definitely got more time to be fleshed out which is amazing, however it was a lot less corny and silly than the last which is why i liked the first. still not bad

Last Watched: March 26th, 2025

title: Spider-Man
review:
peter is so much stronger than me bro if the girl i was madly in love with since 4th grade made out with me and told me she loved me i am noooooooot holding out that’s wife now. but good film norman was really good in this as a villain, and as far as origin stories go they got this right

Last Watched: March 26th, 2025

title: Daredevil
review:
brutal watch. everything was super poorly done and the story they TRIED to tell was incredibly rushed. the only redeeming part of the film was how corny and so bad its good bullseye was. the kingpin thing was a very last minute addition, and we had no time to care about literally anything. the fight scenes were also just really bad.

Last Watched: March 27th, 2025

title: X2: X-Men United
review:
really really good i thought, everything about the film was great. good intro to nightcrawler, logan and jean had some great moments. erik and charles are the winners every single time. their chemistry is unbridled, and their acting is just phenomenal. magneto himself is a great villain, extremely consistent with how he feels towards mutants and humans, willing to help when it benefits him and willing to change paths for his personal beliefs when he shouldn’t. he is just a wonderful villain. the only thing missing to make this perfect is the emotional aspect, i feel like i didn’t connect with some of the sad moments portrayed

Last Watched: March 28th, 2025

title: Spider-Man 2
review:
i really really enjoyed this movie, emotionally you feel horrible for peter because of how spider-men are always cursed. MJ does a wonderful job acting in this and her character makes so much sense with the choices she makes that you feel so bad for her even if you know why peter has to do this. i feel like doc ock was given a really good amount of time to breathe and his arc made a lot of sense. harry at the end has gone full mad and its left me excited for that third movie

Last Watched: March 28th, 2025

title: Blade: Trinity
review:
the action is quite average and the film itself doesn’t seem to really tell a story that progresses it stays the same for ages until its boss fight time, but ryan reynolds jokes were awful and the comedy as a whole was nowhere near silly or corny as it should be for a film like this. it wasn’t funny, or action packed. it was just there

Last Watched: March 29th, 2025

title: Elektra
review:
it simply cant get worse than this. there was a story, but the flow for everything together is just so badly done and every scene felt like it was either reshot or misplaced. i don't know the story behind why this film is so bad but quite literally all of it sucked, the only redeeming thing for me was the nice relationship between elektra and abbey and it not feeling weirdly placed, but everything else suuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuucked. whats the point.

Last Watched: March 31st, 2025

title: Fantastic Four
review:
i don’t think this is a bad film by any means, i was actually quite impressed. the cgi and abilities do look wank but that’s because it’s 2005. the casting was pretty good, i wasn’t a fan of reed richards but the thing and johnny storm were class. specific the thing, emotionally he was the best part of this film and you resonate with him a lot. the final fight scene was weak, and doom as a whole didn’t feel like that big of a deal. pretty fun though

Last Watched: April 3rd, 2025

title: X-Men: The Last Stand
review:
so much happened here and so much of it got reversed with the end credit scene and last scene from the movie 😭 if we were gonna be ballsy we gotta stick to that direction. the movie itself was fine, a lot of shocking moments and stuff that felt rushed like magneto getting the brotherhood together and almost everything with jean. the action was decent, not that much fight scenes up until the final battle and even then not many people had a direct shine. emotionally, this movie resonated well. magneto stole the show yet again, he is perfectly flawed.

Last Watched: April 9th, 2025

title: Ghost Rider
review:
i honestly was shocked at how much i enjoyed this. the villains sucked and the CGI was quite atrocious (almost understandable for the time) but the comedy and action did make up for it at times. johnnys and roxanne’s relationship was pretty good, and i enjoyed the slade review. story felt rushed and felt there was more needed, but still just really fun at times

Last Watched: April 27th, 2025

title: Spider-Man 3
review:
this is a good movie but following the absolute classic that’s the second one, having three somewhat fleshed out villains, and weird pacing i can see why it’s the lease liked. its extremely iconic, bad boy parker walking around NYC dancing was absolutely hilarious and he had some banger quotes. i think id have not included the sandman like at all, he did nothing for me in this and the film almost very much would’ve been the same if it was just focused on harry and eddie. mj deserves better tho

Last Watched: April 26th, 2025

title: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer
review:
not much about this film was particularly good. johnny very much carried the whole movie i thought, chris evans got to shine. there was no action i don’t think until the last 20 ish minutes, and it was fine at best. the doom and johnny fight was fun, everything else wasn’t. i also really did enjoy the silver surfer, one of the coolest marvel characters. didn’t like galactus’ portrayal in this

Last Watched: April 26th, 2025

title: Iron Man
review:
tony’s power has always been his brain rather than the suit, and i think this is one of the only films to really nail that home perfectly. obadiah was very very intimidating in his one on one scenes with tony and pepper, and i thought a pretty good villain all-throughout

Last Watched: February 2nd, 2025

title: The Incredible Hulk
review:
i went into this expecting it to be absolutely awful because that’s how it was made out to be, but honestly there were some parts this was solid. not the greatest movie still, but a lot better than i did expect it to be. i will say that blonsky kinda stole the show for me, i found him to be super captivating. i do think recasting bruce with mark ruffalo was a good decision but edward norton wasn’t awful, just didn’t show how much of a scientist bruce is

Last Watched: February 24th, 2025

title: X-Men Origins: Wolverine
review:
as a stand alone movie it’s actually quite nice, but knowing the story going in and what will happen just doesn’t leave any suspense or doubt. the twists it had did genuinely shock me. i do like the overall concept though, it’s really interesting to learn just how old logan is and how he got to where he is at. i’m not sure if this deadpool is the same deadpool we see in those movies, but if it is i’m really interested to see how we get there. emma frost cameo was super cool. if i had a nickel for every movie that had a version of deadpool, wolverine, and gambit…

Last Watched: April 28th, 2025

title: Iron Man 2
review:
i thought this was exceptional, almost just as good as the first. was both action pact and gave a spotlight onto tony’s humanism as well as his intellect

Last Watched: February 24th, 2025

title: Thor
review:
it’s not boring but it’s not extremely exciting either. i always forgot about thors friend group as they’re incredibly one dimensional characters that never have any individuality. loki, of course, was the star of the show

Last Watched: March 5th, 2025

title: X-Men: First Class
review:
i was thoroughly shocked at how much I liked this. i am never really a big fan of prequels, mainly because it can make what we have already seen feel lesser or not add up. there are some moments like that in this, but the acting and story alone were just really good. the younger Charles and Erik ate the acting up. their chemistry, delivery, etc were just amazing and extremely compelling. they are def gay lovers I will not listen to any other suggestions

Last Watched: April 29th, 2025

title: Captain America: The First Avenger
review:
very very straightforward and simple in the best and most fun way. captain america came off likable in every scene, exactly what you would want and expect. skinny steve rodgers is always super funny to me, and I thought that peggy and steves relationship was built incredibly well

Last Watched: March 13th, 2025

title: Ghost Rider: Spirit of Vengeance
review:
man what happened from the first one into this one? every bit of character was stripped away from johnny and mephisto. the plot made absolutely zero sense and the film itself was just bonkers. i don’t even know if the action scenes along warrant the half a star im giving this. the cgi for ghost rider was bad, the entire story sucked. johnny blaze didn’t feel like the same guy i watched from the last film. they even offer him a chance to “rid his curse” and he goes along with it despite being given that option as an out and choosing to keep his powers to help people last movie. it just makes no sense

Last Watched: April 29th, 2025

title: The Avengers
review:
it’s always so fun seeing how they interacted and met each other at first. i think this is the best the hulk character ever gets in the mcu, and enjoyed how he was presented and acted. i also just love knowing they all prove each other or themselves wrong about any doubts throughout the next movies, very fun storytelling and a lot of foreshadowing i’m glad they they delivered on

Last Watched: March 14th, 2025

title: The Amazing Spider-Man
review:
honestly i thought andrew had the weakest of the spider-men coming into this and I was kinda shocked at how much I enjoyed this. the only thing keeping this from being higher is the lizard overall. he looked meh and the fights between him and peter were meh. there wasn't much action in the film, but besides that I think andrews spider-man is honestly quite good. from the three of them, I feel like andrews gets the most "spider-man" quips and wise remarks. i can understand some peoples complaints about him being too hot to be peter parker are eh, like yeah he is quite attractive, but he is sooo awkward and nerdy that it does even out in my opinion. peter and gwen had a lot of chemistry it felt like, really enjoy their relationship. fun movie hopefully the second is just as good

Last Watched: April 30th, 2025

title: Iron Man 3
review:
i went into this thinking it was the weakest but it really won me over during the watch. his chemistry with harley was very funny, and for most of the movie he isn't in suit so he has to use his intellect. the only weak part about it to me was the madarin storyline merging with killian, it just feels really weird as a misdirect.

Last Watched: March 14th, 2025

title: The Wolverine
review:
the end credit scence got this an extra half star, but the story itself was super.. random? i felt like. i enjoyed the bits showcasing logans ptsd and desire to finally die and rest. those story beats and his mental health showcasing was really really good. the entire story with the yakuza however was not good to me. i felt like it was super out of place, and did NOT tie into to that overall story of his mental health no matter how hard they tried to shoehorn it in. however I did enjoy yukio she was really fun

Last Watched: May 2nd, 2025

title: Thor: The Dark World
review:
almost everything about this film was quite brutal. it was dark for so much of it that i couldn’t barely see it, the dark elves were extremely boring and not really portrayed all that well, and a lot of stuff happens here that never get touched up on again within earth when you definitely feel like it would be. the thing that very much comes to mind now is that it’s so boring and inconsequential that i don’t remember much from it from my original watch, and i don’t remember much from it and i just finished the film 5 minutes ago. just boring.

Last Watched: March 15th, 2025

title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier
review:
i love action packed movies so much, and the fight scenes in this were absolutely on it. the inclusion of sam wilson and bringing bucky back add so much to the cap franchise and this was such a great story to bring them both in.

Last Watched: March 16th, 2025

title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
review:
the only reason it’s getting this high of a review is the chemistry between gwen and peter. their relationship makes a lot of sense and you feel for both of them through their acting. the final moment with them two is just heart breaking. the action and story itself was very bad, the best action scene was funnily enough the one cut off by the end credits. there was way too much trying to be cramped into the film that nothing felt fleshed out and even decent

Last Watched: May 2nd, 2025

title: X-Men: Days of Future Past
review:
this movie has blown me away. visually it looks phenomenal, the scene with quicksilver running around the kitchen, a football stadium being lifted, depicting a dark but believable future. i’m blown away by how good the story was, it made sense and was very emotionally compelling not knowing genuinely what would happen. magneto, younger this time, stole the show again. his character is so beautifully flawed. the only thing that holds it back is a time travel movie, hate time travel and the concept, but for what it was this was a good time travel movie.

Last Watched: May 2nd, 2025

title: Guardians of the Galaxy
review:
everything about this movie is almost absolute perfect. i think for me to give it a full 5 stars, id want mantis there as she adds an extra element that i believe was missing in this one. other than that, absolutely hands down a phenomenal movie from all fronts.

Last Watched: March 16th, 2025

title: Daredevil Season 1
review: i knew this show was very good going into it but im stunned at just how good it honestly was. the acting is impeccable, the show genuinely felt like a show, and the story was remarkable. the action of course is good, and i love the realism of how almost half of the show you see matt being stitched up or bruised. it gives it such a street level hero feel that i don’t think is necessary replicated often. the one thing holding this season back from a 5 is i feel like the middle episodes lulled a bit and it slowed down midway through. but not fully a bad thing, helped the story a little, but still present.

title: Jessica Jones Season 1
review: kilgrave is a top 5 mcu villain of all time and i wish that more people would watch this show to know it. every scene he’s in you find him more and more disturbing and awful. he isn’t intimidating by power, but just by mind control. its perfect. every character in this show had a great story, malcom, trish, even hope. luke cages involvement was great, and i can’t wait to see his show. jessica herself is amazing, she conveys the trying to be strong but so scared about being stalked energy flawlessly. as a show i thought it was fun, its more of a detective show than anything and i love it all. the one thing that was weak was the final fight with kilgrave, admittedly it would be hard to make him seem strong now, but i just felt like he could’ve used the powers he was training to be stronger a little better. very fun

title: Avengers: Age of Ultron
review:
it sadly is the weakest of the avenger films (so far) but that doesn't mean its a bad film. there are more things with this film that I definitely am just not that big of a fan of, like how easily they were able to beat ultron, most of the action scenes, and killing quicksilver in his mcu debut. a speedster shouldn't be able to get shot, like at all, he is supposed to be faster than that. the comedy also fell flat. i just think there were spots to improve, but as a movie its really fun and captivating. also banner and natasha felt out of nowhere, but I understand why they needed to do it. fun thanos tease at the end though

Last Watched: March 17th, 2025

title: Ant-Man
review:
ant man is so incredibly fun. it is very funny throughout and the take on the origin story stands out in a really good way. the final fight is always the star of the show for me, its so unique and exciting how they use the shrinking and growing concept within a childs room

Last Watched: March 17th, 2025

title: Fantastic Four (2015)
review:
wow i knew this film was bad going into it, but i had no idea it was THIS bad. every actor was an awful casting choice, the story was super boring and not enjoyable. the action was very bad. everything about the film was bad. if letterboxd ever lets me write 0 star reviews just know this film isn’t safe

Last Watched: May 2nd, 2025

title: Deadpool
review:
very very funny and amazing action scenes. i also think the relationship between wade and vanessa was very well done and actually made for some nice emotional moments. the villain was a bit meh and some of the comedy definitely undercut the serious moments, but that’s kinda the point of the movie. really cool opener too

Last Watched: May 13th, 2025

title: Daredevil Season 2
review: this was such a good season. the inclusions of punisher and elektra really made this season feel 1000% more marvel. everything surrounding the punisher was great, and i really loved the courtroom scene with him. nobu being the main target was really really smart and i enjoyed all of it, and the blacksmith reveal genuinely did shock me. very down to earth and fun

title: Captain America: Civil War
review:
i love this film so much. just the fact that it’s genuinely split its audience as well, a moral dilemma that makes people choose. the action was impeccable, and i thought it served as a great introduction to the black panther and spider-man. plus seeing all the silly heroes fight reminds me of marvel rivals and that’s fun

Last Watched: March 18th, 2025

title: X-Men: Apocalypse
review:
i'd have preferred if the follow up to days of future past at least followed up. this showcases the mutant abilities in a really cool way, but the timeline that was already confusing enough got more confusing for me here, and the actual movie itself was quite boring so it didn't really help. the quicksilver scene was super cool, and I am a big fan of how magentos powers were done, but outside of that I didn't like much of it.

Last Watched: May 19th, 2025

title: Luke Cage Season 1
review: the show is good but it’s definitely a drop in quality from the last two. it had started very slow, then definitely picked up about halfway through. once it picked up it kept it up until like, episode 10 where it started to drag a bit. the final fight with diamondback was a bit meh and the ending felt odd. shoutout claire tho

title: Doctor Strange
review:
so so visually beautiful and breathtaking. stephen himself grows on you throughout the movie, and he does impress within the fights. i think the final fight is super unique within marvel, just looping itself

Last Watched: March 18th, 2025

title: Legion Season 1
review: i love shows about mental health, and i love shows that confuse me and make me thing. this was the PERFECT merge between them. i absolutely adore syd and david’s relationship, i think they are perfect for each other and if anything ever happens to them i will personally be the most angry human being alive. david himself is such a fascinating character, i love their portrayal of schizophrenia and drug abuse and how people handle mental health issues. the side characters were alright, and i loved the ending and post credit scene, i’m honestly excited for when i get to pick this back up.

title: Logan
review:
what a beautiful movie. old logan and old Charles just trying to survive together and logan doing his best to keep Charles there since he has always been the best of logan. it just shows how much the professor always meant to him. laura was a great addition, and her and logans chemistry together was amazing. being rated r definitely helped the fight scenes and acting, some of the gore actually reminded me of the boys, and I wonder if they got ideas from this. the concept of logan simply just getting too old instead of any cure like what was proposed in the wolverine is amazing, and this is the perfect send off to logan overall. the x grave because he is THE x-man too. masterful.

Last Watched: June 2nd, 2025

title: Iron Fist Season 1
review: it’s definitely nowhere near as exciting as the other defender season 1’s. i don’t think danny rand himself was all that entertaining, but the situations he was in was entertaining. colleen wing was by far my most favorite character, and i think my second was ward. ward had such a good arch and felt genuine. i kind of wish the hand was this season, and that next season was harold. just felt like both in one season was a lot and didn’t give both stories a good chance to breathe. i am intrigued to see where this goes from here but not as excited as i was for literally any of the other defenders

title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
review:
regardless of the usual comedy being good and character work perfected for the guardians movie, the film itself was visually appealing and the added elements of baby groot, mantis, a funnier drax, and a nebula all led to just better quality. i love the yondu story and ending, and i thought ego was a really really good villain.

Last Watched: March 19th, 2025

title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
review:
it’s fun, if i’m honest the shine for me was vulture but purely keeping his attitude that he had when he was alone with peter in the car. i thought that scene was extremely intense and if he was presented as a dude who’d do anything to provide for his family i’d have enjoyed or cared about the final fight a bit more. peter and ned are good, liz was class but i’m happy we of course ended up with MJ. not the best spidey film ever but still a good one

Last Watched: March 20th, 2025

title: The Defenders Season 1
review: maybe i set my expectations too high, or maybe i was just expecting the same feeling i had when i watched the avengers for the first time and they’re all together, but this was not that. the first few episodes of them coming together were alright, but once the hand became the pivotal plot point, and then elektra killed then and became the villain, it lost me entirely. i also don’t particularly think iron fist being the main character almost was a great idea considering the show itself was bad, and i just didn’t enjoy his chemistry with the group. jessica jones and matt murdock together were great, jessica and luke again was really nice, but danny just stands out in a bad way. i’m sad they only got one season for this show, i wish they could’ve developed some characters more like they did daredevil before this, maybe it’d have been better.

title: The Inhumans Season 1
review: if i had a nickel for every time Iwan Rheon played a kingdom usurper and tortured a ginger woman.. this show i feel like had a good idea in mind? maybe? but in execution it was just dreadfully boring. some of the characters were alright but they weren't built on or expanded past their general personalities shown at the VERY start of the show and that is the entire role they played. i feel like at a show level this was not really a show, and they could've maybe benefited from being a movie, or an actual show that had seasons worth of build rather than episode 1 or 2 starting the entire storyline that wraps by episode 8. nothing shocked me, no consequences really happened because the one death ended up being reversed anyways. losing attalan would've been cool to see as a whole except that happened with 3 minutes left and you don't get to see the follow up, probably because a second season was cancelled, but i am not shocked that it was. boring, but heart in the right place, i guess.

title: Thor: Ragnarok
review:
this movie is so incredibly fun. it’s very well paced, funny at the right time, hela as a villain was threatening, and the final fight was incredibly entertaining. still to this day do i think skurge is gonna say “oi hughie”. weird seeing loki still trying to betray thor because we just seem so past that atp

Last Watched: March 20th, 2025

title: The Punisher Season 1
review: it started off incredibly slow, when it picked up it didn’t hold back. i thought ebon moch killed it this and stole every scene he was in, and all the characters were built up incredibly. the punisher himself was very good, everything felt true to the character.

title: The Runaways Season 1
review: honestly super cute, the romance in it was built very well, and the story picked up after about episode 3. the main complaints i have is making SOME of the parents redeemable when they’ve been awful the whole time towards the end, and the weak final fight/moments. the last episode was definitely an episode that didn’t feel like it had that conclusive of an ending, but wanted to lead into something. i appreciate it planning for the future, but it did NOT feel like a season finale. the group dynamic and chemistry is very good though, i was thoroughly surprised by the show. phenomenal usage of the song ocean eyes too

title: Black Panther
review:
i wish at some point we spent more learning about the tradition and culture of wakanda more, because in terms of world building they made it to vastly unique and interesting. killmonger is a villain for once i wish got to be kept alive to become a hero, i felt like all of his presentation was remarkable, and he’s as justified as he could be with what he wanted. so justified that t’challa just does what killmonger set out to do. i love this movie so much, each casting was perfect.

Last Watched: March 21st, 2025

title: Jessica Jones Season 2
review: the start to this was pretty slow compared to the prior seasons for it, and i don’t think the storyline in particular was as strong as it could’ve been. the reveal of the real villain was really cool, but im sad at how predictable it was overall. trish and malcom really shined this season, especially malcom. their arcs were really enjoyable to watch, and im glad they got time to stand on their own. jerri also owned this season, and i am excited to see what’s next for her and malcom. really i just don’t think the villain was all that good; and the way we got there was ultimately disappointing and obvious. i think the acting itself and its pacing was very fun, however, which does set it above the rest. every story besides the main story was great

title: Legion Season 2
review: this season is so much more heartbreaking. i felt absolutely gutted at some of the decisions, but in an intrigued and emotional way. the main thing i loved about season one was the relationship between syd and david, and this turns it on so many different heads and adds so many layers i don’t know who’s side i’m on, or how to feel. david himself having so many different variants, and showing how it’s either full evil or dead and zombified… just insane. i am gagged at the direction the season went; and even more surprised at how it ended.

title: Avengers: Infinity War
review:
everything they could do they did correctly in this movie. the teams were perfectly split, and never once felt uneven. the guardians got an amazing shine here, practically having the upper hand on both ironman and spider-man. the pacing for this movie is incredibly good, we don’t stay with one group too long and we set up multiple times where you’d think they’d have thanos beat. the thor storyline was practically right there, and it’s fair to assume that he’d be the closing piece, and then he comes up just a little short. vision and wanda DO beat thanos and destroy the stone, but of course he uses the time and puts their efforts back to none. i love movies where the villains, and i love movies where villains are respectful and justified; thanos embodies this. more franchises should have villains win, not every issue is a one movie kind of issue

Last Watched: March 21st, 2025

title: Deadpool 2
review:
i enjoyed the film a lot, honestly i think it’s kind of funnier than the first. the scene with all of the x-men for a full second had me cackling, and i thought the actual story element behind the film was pretty good. the action scenes and music was also remarkably fun.

Last Watched: October 7th, 2025

title: Luke Cage Season 2
review: this was a REALLY good season. luke cage being corrupted by harlem itself being the underlying narrative and the final episode just wrapped it all together so wonderfully. bushmaster was a good villain, and i’m glad that shades and mariah had a lot of time to really shine this season. i fully expected mariah to be the villain next season, so the final episode caught me entirely off guard. i also think that this is how a show should handle cameos, danny rand, colleen, foggy all did WONDERFUL and felt so refreshing and enjoyable to see. this was the best iron fist has been so far, and foggy felt genuinely important throughout this. it’s sad jessica jones s2 didn’t have this, but this felt like a real follow up to the defenders, even mentioning matt a bit. i’m sad this is the last luke cage season.

title: Cloak & Dagger Season 1
review: honestly took me off guard with how good it was! i kind of felt more like one of the netflix shows with its pacing and how it told its story, i thought roxxon corp was a perfect villain and the character motives made a lot of sense behind it. both tandy and ty felt very well cast, especially ty with how he delivers his lines. i do wish there was more about figuring out their physical powers rather than the mental abilities of hope and fear, as well as a bit more actual fight scenes. the final fight felt really weird considering how minimal of fighting they actually did. also, i’d love to see them getting together next season. they very much have great chemistry together, i do like evita but just can’t wait

title: Ant-Man and the Wasp
review:
this was super incredibly mid, not good but not bad. just there. ghost was cool but I felt like she was a bit underdeveloped, the FBI and the black market dealers being involved tried to cover ghost being weak but were also weak themselves. luis kind of stole the show in the movie with his lines and character, but other than that it was just a movie. cool end credit scene and the post-credit scene could be really cool if the ant wasn't drumming, just showing a quiet and empty room with the tv on a global emergency screen.

Last Watched: March 22nd, 2025

title: Iron Fist Season 2
review: i will say story wise it’s significantly better than season 1, but it’s still incredibly boring. it’s never a good sign when the mantle needs to be passed to someone else because your main character is so incredibly unlikable and annoying in all aspects. i am not a writer but how can you make danny rand incredibly likable and figured out in luke cage but not this season. every character that wasn’t danny was fun, but one of my favorites was ward again. his arc this season was very enjoyable, and his acting was very good and made you connect. knowing that this is the end of iron fist that we see, the ending is a bit weird, and i don’t really know how you follow that up at all.

title: Venom
review:
really fun film, i thought the way that the symbiote was explained was good, and that the dynamic between him and eddie was quite good. i do wish we got a bit more fight scenes that weren’t venom vs other venom, but the way he was presented in the smoke like a horror villain was great. i thought he also looked quite visually impressive, good presentation and good execution behind it, the carnage tease was extremely fun too

Last Watched: October 8th, 2025

title: Daredevil Season 3
review: first thing i have watched apart of this marvel rewatch that has made me want to break the 5 star scale, if letterboxd or serializd let me, i would. there are so many episodes within this season i watched just taken aback by how good the visuals and action are. the main episode i wanted to point out specifically is episode 4, the action take inside of the prision and the colors flashing all over a zoomed in matt was off the charts remarkable. the story itself with bullseye, kingpin, and nadeem was so wonderfully told too. nadeem was built entirely well, giving his character time and fleshing him out as well as an episode dedicated to bullseye's backstory is how you make each character feel just as important. obviously, matt, karen, and foggy were all treated as equals despite the show being about matt. karen had the most time to shine i thought, even an episode titled about her and dedicated to her (i thought she was a goner). EVERY character had a great arc and had wonderful emotional decisions and they were very logic driven. everything in this season was given time to breathe and they executed it all perfectly. this is the netflix series' magnum opus.

title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
review:
a perfect example of animation. unique style for EACH universe and character animated on separate frame rates. visually, this movie may be up there with the very best. vibrant, colorful, stunning. the comic book animation with dialog boxes and text that FOLLOWS the character is just a cherry on top. genuinely a touching story with characters you care about and invest in, with beautiful scenes that have great songs to accompany it. it’s just the best there is out there

Last Watched: August 6th, 2025

title: The Runaways Season 2
review: if this ranking was based on the second half of this show alone it’d be incredibly higher up, but the starting first 5 or 6 ish episodes dragged so incredibly hard. remarkable ending with the cliffhanger, and the story was entirely unpredictable. i still love gert and chases relationship and want to see them be happy together for just once. karolina and niko deserve the world and im sick of the show splitting them up in a good way. alex did frustrate me a bit this season, as well as the entire topher storyline. but awol and that story with pride was remarkable, i wish it lasted longer, and the final episode was off the charts. i’m really excited to see how cloak and dagger play into next season, and how the group gets back together.

title: The Punisher Season 2
review: the half is purely because jon bernthal's presentation of the punisher is just out of this world. this season fell incredibly flat for more. it had an impeccable start, i love the dynamic between amy and frank, and i wish that was the only story for the season. it felt nice seeing frank as a protector, and i thought that they had good chemistry. this season fell off for me when it came to billy, i think the entire inclusion of that arc was not needed for me, and kind of interrupted the flow because i just didn't care about billy after last season at all. i did, however, really like john pilgrim and thought that was a very unique and interesting villain, and i thought it was portrayed very captivatingly. shame the wrong story got prioritized, and there was just too much happening at once. maybe it couldve been split into different seasons, or one story for half the season and the other story for the remaining, who knows.

title: Captain Marvel
review:
i would say that the best part of the film was goose by a mile, but I do like the misdirect with the kree and skrull war. i didn't think brie was particularly great nor bad, just kinda there. i think fury getting to show more character was fun. its just a film, its not the worst movie ever but its a bit above average for me

Last Watched: March 23rd, 2025

title: Cloak & Dagger Season 2
review: this season was sadly not a good follow up to its first, and it makes me sad the show ended here. i was very much looking forward to ty and tandy actually getting a relationship developed on screen, and while we took baby steps there, thats all we did. it felt like we had to keep them apart specifically to make sure they did not get together, because they have remarkable chemistry and its also the divine pairing cloak and dagger, so obviously they should be together. the story itself was fine, servicable most parts i would say, the villian was cool and had interesting mind tricks but the episode where tandy went through multiple realities may be one of the worst episodes of tv i have ever watched. it felt incredible out of place and like it was genuine filler. i wish to see tandy and ty again, but sadly this is the end for them.

title: Avengers: Endgame
review:
the movie is amazing, but the most GLARING thing that bothers me is thanos. this isn’t the thanos we had last movie. sure, that thanos won and is done, but this thanos doesn’t show the same level of respect whilst talking to the other heroes. another thing keeping it away from there is time travel being the solution and how it was explained. i just don’t like time travel movies, i understand it was needed obviously, but it’s still something that feels almost like a cop out. the humor was weirdly placed as well. on the positive side, emotionally and visually this movie gets a lot of it right. they started on iron man, very much telling where the movie was going to go, and the ONE time he didn’t want to have the fight he needed to. visually, the ronin scene and the final fight with every avenger both look so amazing. ronin the rain adding to the atmosphere and the neon lights shining gave so much life to that one specific scene that it stands out. the final fight being so dark but yet everyone stands out, it’s just very visually good. the story is fun, and the characters get to tie up their lose ends and progress the story well. 

long review because it’s end game, obviously.

Last Watched: March 23rd, 2025

title: Jessica Jones Season 3
review: man that first season really was special wasn't it. i thought most of the season was simply fine and servicable. i am a big fan of crime shows so a serial killer that doesn't have powers as the villian was great, and the trish storyline made sense despite i think being rushed. i didnt care for erik, didnt enjoy how where we left season 2 off essentially didnt matter, and wasnt a fan of jeri practically regressing this season. i think her regression does make sense, but at the same time was frustrating to watch in a bad way. luke cage showing up was cool, even if he is the kingpin of harlem, i hope to see him soon again. not sure how they will cover post defenders jessica jones with her return coming up

title: X-Men: Dark Phoenix
review:
this is one of the most boring movies i’ve seen. and it’s unfortunately not funny bad, it’s just boring bad so it feels more exhausting to watch. it’s not really the actors faults either, it’s just how rushed the story was compared to the new cast of actors and how weirdly written it was. james mcavoy and fassbender are just so good, perfect follows to ian mckellen and patrick stewart, it’s a shame what their movies gave them. very unfortunate end to the x-men film lineup and not really shocked it was the last.

Last Watched: December 3rd, 2025

title: Legion Season 3
review: i'm so sad i didn't enjoy this anywhere near as i did the first two seasons. i was of course very curious how youd follow up on david after the season 2 finale, and i understand that it was a hard task but it just fell so flat for me. i have always been a believer of your past doesn't define you, and it's sad to see them leaning into the opposite of essentially too much happened we have to restart the timeline with time travel. again, i get it was a hard wall their backs were against, but i just don't feel like i am fully satisfied with that ending. i am happy to see some characters like syd and kerry get some nice endings, and the episode of oliver and melanie raising syd was really cute. visually and musically, this show is still untouchable. some great scenes and even better choices and usage of music to convey a message. i wish this wasn't the end.

title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
review:
a lot about this movie is super bad. mysterio being more of an iron man villain sucks, peter giving the glasses up to him making him look like such an idiot. sure he’s a kid, but this presentation of peter is nowhere near my favorite. the visuals on this movie are amazing, and mj and peter’s relationship was pretty well done, but everything else was just not good.

Last Watched: March 24th, 2025

title: The Runaways Season 3
review: they threw three seasons of tv into this one season and it felt like it. the first four eps dedicated to the alien plot was fine, odd yes but it was a good pickup on where we last left off. the following dark dimension plot felt incredibly weird, and did not feel like it had concluded the alien story but somehow did. the wizard plot felt rushed, and could have used time to develop the feelings and story of the kids and pride working in tandem. the time travel episode was incrediblyyyyyyyy unneeded and made everything that we just watched feel so unnecessary and weird. there were nice moments and all but this is a very bad finish to the show. i dont believe we ever see any of the runaways again, even if we are to expect cloak and dagger to return (which i enjoyed their involvement in this season for as small as it was). if we do, id like to see nico back since we explore witches in agatha.

title: The New Mutants
review:
yikes this is worse than i thought it’d be. it doesn’t feel genuine at all idk how to describe it. i don’t think any of their chemistry was all too good and i think the writing and story were just incredibly generic. the characters weren’t all that memorable probably besides magik, and the doctor was super easy to see coming. the worst part is being bored. a bad movie can be fun if it’s funny bad, this was just boring bad.

Last Watched: February 15th, 2026

title: WandaVision
review:
what a perfect start to post endgame (besides spider-man) and the disney+ tv shows as a whole. i’ve said so many times that marvel is at its best when it’s backed by actual emotional elements and trauma and the whole show was based on just that. every character was played to actual perfection. i think the involvement of side characters from past projects was also such a huge help because we already like and resonate with darcy, jimmy, and monica and putting them in made sense and really still felt like a connected world. the only negative i have is the fact a final fight even had to happen, i think emotionally the story would’ve worked WITHOUT a big fight, and just exploring emotions would’ve been fun. each episode was very unique, and i just really enjoyed the vibes from all of them.

Last Watched: February 18th, 2026

episodes.title: Episode 1: Filmed Before a Live Studio Audience
Episode 5: On a Very Special Episode...

title: The Falcon and The Winter Soldier Season 1
review:
it’s definitely a fine show. i’ve been firm in the belief that it’d had been better as a movie, because it feels like it was shot like one. the action was good, and sam, bucky, john walker, and zemo were INCREDIBLE. unfortunately the flag smashers felt flat because they were kinda right for the most part, and k think the threat of them was unnecessary as a whole when it should’ve been more focused on sam and his struggle taking the cap mantle. i liked the story i just feel like this would’ve been a much better brand new day than the one we got, and that splitting this into 6 episodes only hurt it.

Last Watched: February 25th, 2026

episodes.title: Episode 4: The Whole World is Watching

title: Loki Season 1
review: what a great season of television. the score and set design of the entire show just sets it apart and makes you want to look and listen at every little detail. i love every new character, slyvie and mobius just steal the show entirely. loki is wonderful in this, and his arc is done so beautifully. the selfcest thing is kinda weird but makes sense at the same time. i love the tva as a concept and love seeing the inner workings of it. shame what happened to that he who remains guy

title: Black Widow
review:
honestly it’s quite unfortunate that this movie ended up the way it did. for the only black widow solo project i don't necessarily mind it being a prequel, but there are so many other stories i would’ve liked to have been told. i just feel
like the story was incredibly formulaic and boring at times. the film had a hot start and good action and only went downhill from there, bar any yelena or alexei scenes. i don’t necessarily understand why taskmaster was thrown into this, and it didn’t feel like there was ever any struggle or tension, just felt flat for me

Last Watched: March 18th, 2026

title: What If...? Season 1
review: i know people dislike this show, but this season is incredibly good. it has its meh moments for sure, like the party episode and killmonger episode, but the highs are impeccable. the doctor strange episode is one of my favorite animated episodes of any television series, visually and thematically it’s just great storytelling. the ultron episode, to me, is so cool. i am such a big ultron fan that not only having him win, but having him be a multiversal level threat was so fun to me. the scene where he can hear the watcher and breaks through will always make me smile. the only thing that keeps me from rating this higher is the unfortunate circumstances. guardians of the multiverse is really cool, but in a show where the premise was random alternate changes and unique moments, having all of the characters from that season come together at the end to face the big bad and the watcher to break his oath on the very first season isn’t great. the next two seasons do suck yeah, but they were always doomed to fail because of this being done NOW rather than at the end of the show. nothing could possibly live up to this and now that the watcher already broke his oath, what’s really the point? it’s still fun, and it still has absolute gem banger episodes but that keeps it out of the top tier for me

title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
review:
i knew nothing about this moving going into it other than the music slaps, and that did not disappoint nor did much else. the action is impeccable, the bus fight scene being probably one of marvels best. i’m glad they included trevor and mentioned the mandarin, the comedy moments were very strong in this movie. my only main gripe is wenwu sending shang chi to kill the guys who killed his mom rather than himself. it doesn’t make sense he’s willing to invade somewhere and destroy it over thinking he’ll have her back, but not go and kill the guy responsible for it. i also didn’t necessarily expect a soul eating dragon to be the villain, i liked wenwu and would’ve been alright if he wasn’t redeemable. but everything else was genuinely off the charts. if katy and shang chi don’t end up together i may have to sue someone i liked their chemistry and thought they were fun together. the fight scenes just steal the show, i could watch them fight with the hype beat music behind it all day

Last Watched: March 29th, 2026

title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
review:
it was a pretty alright film. nothing was overly great or overly bad. i enjoyed eddie and venoms banter, and the undertones of them being in a gay relationship with venom at a rave was pretty fun, venom is out of the eddie closet happy pride month. i wouldnt say carnage had the most time to breath and develop as a character, but i dont think there was much time for anything as the film was very short and everything felt rushed. by the time we got to the end fight i was shocked we were already there, but it wasnt bad. cool end credit scene too, cant wait to see it followed up on!

Last Watched: June 19th, 2026

title: Eternals
review:
honestly a really good film, just wrong place wrong time, wrong format? its not a unique take to say that i think the movie would have benefited more from being a show, rather than telling us their history or having flashbacks, have seasons that show us their past and establish their relationships and dynamics before killing half and the other half having an eternal civil war. another thing is the placement of when the film came out, the fourth movie after endgame AND being one of the only movies since then to really mention what happened with thanos and the blip, i think it was just poor timing to try to weave them into the story AFTER all of that. i would even go as far as to say the eternals outright asking who will be in charge of the avengers after took me out of their own story a bit, because it just doesn't make sense for them to try to connect it that strong and also try to explain why they haven't helped in the same exact moment. on the good, the acting is actually really good, and the premise is very fun, i was left wanting more of almost every eternal, but its a shame that a lot of their story started and ended in the same movie. i hope they do return, and maybe a prequel show would be best after secret wars, but wanting more is a good thing and bad thing sometimes. i was very shocked at how much i did enjoy it, its just one GLARING issue with the movie at the end of the day. cool end credit scene too, cant wait to see it followed up on!

Last Watched: June 23rd, 2026

title: Hawkeye Season 1
review:
honestly, it’s really fun and lighthearted for the most part. i think it does a really, really good job at establishing kate and making hawkeye more enjoyable and feel more interesting than he gets in the avengers movies. i will say it significantly picks up when yelena shows up, and i’d have preferred her to be a more prominent character in more episodes, because ever scene she’s in she is stealing. i think her and kate have really good chemistry, and i hope it’s put on screen more soon. the maya story was more enjoyable this time around, but kingpin left me confused on how he’s able to tank arrows, explosions, AND a gunshot to the face but i haven’t seen the echo show or daredevil born again to explain that just yet. i also like swordsman he is so baby girl coded

Last Watched: June 23rd, 2026

title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
review:
i think people turning on this film after some time are kinda cringe, it’s really good and i enjoy it more with every watch. i watched the extended version and enjoyed the extra daredevil scene, and all of the spider-man dialogue was very fun and im excited to see more of them (hopefully). its really fun getting to watch this after now seeing all of the tobey and andrew movies too, and there are a lot of wonderful character moments. using both tobey and andrew as part of tom’s character development into becoming more like peter parker was really smart, and i’m excited to see what they do with spider-man going forward

Last Watched: July 3rd, 2026

title: The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special
review:
this was my first watch and it is a lot sweeter and fun than i went in assuming it would be. i usually find specials or holiday episodes weird because it warps the characters around the event, but i feel like they actually made this feel incredibly natural and within the regular canon that it worked for me. i do wish we got to see more of the guardians on earth because it was quite funny, and mantis and drax are just so fun to watch. not that much depth to it but its a holiday special so obviously. oh man am i excited to see guardians 3 i cant wait for them all to be so happy and loving like this special :)

Last Watched: June 30th, 2026

title: Captain America: Brave New World
review:
i feel like most of the reviews for this movie are in bad faith. i am keeping it spoiler free, so i will say the movie is action packed and told a good story. you can at some points see what are very obvious reshoots, but that doesn’t take away that badly.
